# claude_federation_rule_workspace (Resource)

Enables a Workload Identity Federation rule in an additional workspace. The rule's initial workspace is set on the `claude_federation_rule` resource (`workspace_id` or `applies_to_all_workspaces`); use this resource to enable the rule in further workspaces. Do not use it together with a rule that has `applies_to_all_workspaces = true`. OAuth-authenticated callers may only manage workspaces of rules whose `oauth_scope` is `workspace:developer` or `workspace:inference`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Enable an existing federation rule in an additional workspace beyond the one
# set on the rule itself.
resource "claude_federation_rule_workspace" "gha_deploy_staging" {
  federation_rule_id = claude_federation_rule.gha_deploy.id
  workspace_id       = "wrkspc_0123456789abcdef"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `federation_rule_id` (String) Identifier of the federation rule (`fdrl_...`). Changing this forces a new resource.
- `workspace_id` (String) Identifier of the workspace (`wrkspc_...`) to enable the rule in. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when the rule was enabled for the workspace.
- `id` (String) Composite identifier in the form `<federation_rule_id>/<workspace_id>`.
- `type` (String) Object type, always `federation_rule_workspace`.
- `workspace_name` (String) Display name of the workspace.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Federation rule workspace enablements can be imported using the composite
# "federation_rule_id/workspace_id" format.
terraform import claude_federation_rule_workspace.gha_deploy_staging fdrl_xxx/wrkspc_xxx
```
